Life_Goal.Description: Create and sustain a tangible cultural-heritage project that preserves the history, techniques, and safety culture of stunt performance in Los Angeles and supports the next generation of performers. Over the next 5–10 years Jackson will: (1) produce a 30–60 minute documentary compiling 10–15 oral histories from veteran stunt performers and key archive materials (target completion: years 2–4); (2) launch an annual two-day "Stunt Heritage & Safety" workshop in Los Angeles that combines historical context, safety clinics, and mentorship, with an initial cohort of ~30 participants in year 3 and growth to 75+ by year 6; and (3) establish a dedicated $25,000 seed fund or donor-advised account (built from a mix of personal allocation, modest fundraising, and sponsorship) by year 5 to provide micro-grants or a small annual scholarship (e.g., $1,500–3,000/year) for training, equipment, or travel for emerging stunt performers. Jackson will fund this sustainably by allocating a defined portion of surplus income/savings (for example 5–10% of annual savings growth or an initial $15,000–30,000 commitment), seeking festival distribution and modest crowdfunding for the documentary, and tracking success with concrete metrics: documentary completion and festival/online distribution, number of workshop participants and instructor partners, number of oral histories archived (target 50+ resources by year 7), and the establishment and disbursement of the seed fund. This goal matters to Jackson because it honors the craft that shaped his career, creates a legacy that improves safety and professional recognition for stunt performers, and gives his creative, values-driven INFP instincts a structured, financially realistic way to invest his earnings into a meaningful, long-term project.

Life_Goal.Description: Become a recognized commercial leader in applying industrial robotics to healthcare within the next 5–10 years. Specific, measurable milestones: (1) Within 18–24 months: lead and close 3 strategic partnerships that integrate robotic solutions into clinical or healthcare operational workflows and generate $3–5M in new revenue for his business unit. (2) Within 3–5 years: be promoted to VP/Head of Robotics Commercialization at his company or co-found a robotics-healthcare startup, secure seed/Series A funding (target $2–10M), and bring at least one commercially viable product or deployed solution to market (pilot deployments in 3–5 healthcare sites). (3) Within 7–10 years: scale the business unit or company to $20–50M annual recurring revenue or achieve an acquisition by a larger medtech firm, build and lead a multidisciplinary team of 20–50 people, and establish 2–4 licensing agreements or IP filings that protect key innovations. Why this matters: it aligns with Paul’s mechanical engineering and MBA background, his ENTP drive for innovation and strategic deal-making, and his desire to create measurable healthcare impact while securing long-term financial and professional stability for himself and his family.

Life_Goal.Description: Create a focused, multi-year cultural heritage project documenting and sharing the stories, traditions, and contemporary life of Cleveland, Tennessee and the surrounding Appalachian community through communications work (oral histories, short documentary films, photo essays, and a searchable digital archive). Over the next 1-2 years, pilot the project by conducting 8–10 recorded interviews with elder family members, local artisans, and community leaders and produce one 8–12 minute short documentary as a portfolio piece. By year 3–5, expand to a core archive of 20+ interviews, 100+ photos and documents, and two short documentaries (10–20 minutes each), launch a simple accessible website to host the materials, and secure at least one partnership with Lee University or the local historical society. By year 6–10, aim to organize a public screening/exhibit in Cleveland (or at Lee University), pursue small grants or community sponsorships to preserve and extend the archive, and explore touring the exhibit to nearby towns or contributing materials to the county historical collection. This goal matters because it aligns with Emily's communications training and INFJ drive for meaningful work: it preserves disappearing personal and local histories, strengthens community connections, builds a concrete communications portfolio for future career options, and provides measurable outputs (number of interviews, films produced, website launched, and public events) to track progress.

Life_Goal.Description: Within the next 5–10 years, develop and scale a practical support program that reduces commuter stress for elementary school communities by combining educational expertise and media skills. Year 1–2: create a branded podcast/video series and accompanying short online curriculum and printable guides aimed at teachers, parents, and students (target: 30–50 episodes or pieces of content in the first 3 years). Year 2–4: run pilot workshops in 5 local schools to test materials and gather feedback; refine resources based on teacher and family needs. Year 4–6: expand reach to at least 20 schools and secure at least one formal partnership or sponsorship (e.g., with a school district, transit agency, or education NGO) to sustain and distribute the program. By year 5–7, transition into a hybrid role that combines media production and education (for example, educational content producer, school well-being coordinator, or program manager) so the work is part of sustainable employment. Alongside program growth, build personal financial stability by increasing savings to cover six months of living expenses. This goal reflects his elementary education background, current media experience, and INFP strengths—using creativity and a desire for harmony to produce concrete, supportive solutions for commuters in school communities.

Preference_Profile.Community_Volunteering: Helps organize the apartment complex cleaning and small events on most weekends (once or twice a month)

Preference_Profile.Coffee_and_Cafes: Visits specialty coffee shops on weekends to try single-origin brews and meet friends (Sat afternoon)

Life_Goal.Description: Over the next 5–10 years, develop and articulate a clear, practice-oriented philosophical framework about the societal, ethical, and governance implications of distributed consensus and decentralization. Concretely, this means: (1) produce a peer-reviewed position paper connecting technical design choices in consensus algorithms to social outcomes within 2 years; (2) design and teach a graduate seminar at the National University of Singapore that integrates technical foundations with ethics and policy, running it at least twice within 5 years; (3) organize an interdisciplinary workshop (computer scientists, ethicists, policymakers, industry) at NUS within 3 years to move ideas toward actionable guidelines; (4) publish a long-form essay or general-audience book within 7 years to communicate these ideas beyond academia; and (5) apply the framework in advising or consulting on at least two real-world projects (industry or public sector) within 8–10 years. This goal matters because it aligns his technical expertise with his ENFP drive for meaningful impact and public engagement, ensuring his research on distributed consensus contributes responsibly to society.

Father.Name: Andrew David

Father.Work_Status: Retired; accepts occasional short-term consultancy work (a few days/month).

Preference_Profile.Gardening: Tends the back garden most mornings and intensively on weekends (at least 4 hours on Saturdays).

Preference_Profile.Historical_Reading: Reads 1–2 historical nonfiction books per month, preferring WWII and industrial history, typically in the evenings.
Preference_Profile.Walking: Takes a brisk 30–45 minute walk daily around the neighborhood and longer countryside walks once a month.
Preference_Profile.Cooking: Enjoys trying a new savory recipe once a month and regularly cooks simple weekend dinners.

Life_Goal.Description: Over the next 5–10 years Anthony will create and sustain a culturally focused literary initiative that preserves and promotes his Cuban‑American heritage and supports emerging Latinx writers. Concrete plan and timeline: (1) Within 12 months launch a hybrid 'Miami Voices' book club that adapts to virtual formats by running quarterly meetings (two virtual, two in‑person annually) and recording sessions for reuse; (2) Within 2 years build a modest website/digital archive to host recorded discussions, author interviews, contextual essays on Cuban‑American literary traditions, and resources for readers and writers; (3) Over 3–5 years curate and edit a bilingual anthology of short stories and essays by Miami‑area Latinx writers, funded through targeted sponsorships and a crowdfunding campaign, with a clear publication timeline; (4) Across 5 years mentor at least six emerging Latinx writers through regular workshops or a small fellowship program. Why this matters: it reconnects him to family roots, turns his struggle with virtual book clubs into leadership and community service, leverages his MFA and executive skills to secure resources, and creates a tangible legacy (ongoing program, digital archive, and published anthology) that is achievable alongside his finance career.

Father.Name: Carlos Rodriguez
Father.Career_Status: Retired (formerly Senior Technician / Public Works machinist for the City of San Diego). Receives a municipal pension and small consulting income from occasional private repair jobs.
Father.Work_Status: Retired — does part-time consulting and home projects on weekends.
Father.Health_Status: Type 2 diabetes (managed with metformin and diet), controlled hypertension, mild osteoarthritis in knees; generally mobile with occasional joint pain.

Preference_Profile.Gardening: Tends a small backyard vegetable and herb garden most mornings, especially Saturdays and Sundays when the weather is mild.
Preference_Profile.Cooking: Enjoys preparing traditional Mexican dishes (mole, tamales) for family gatherings on Sundays and holidays.
Preference_Profile.Watching_Soccer: Watches Liga MX and occasional international matches in the evenings or on weekend afternoons with friends.
Preference_Profile.Woodworking: Works on small woodworking projects in the garage 1–2 evenings per week, prefers simple furniture and repairs.
Preference_Profile.Reading_News: Reads a local Spanish-language newspaper and online news each morning with coffee.

Mother.Name: María Elena Rodriguez

Education_Background.University: Instituto de Ciencias de la Salud (LPN program)

Mother.Career_Status: Retired Registered Practical Nurse (worked at Mercy Hospital and community clinics). Receives pension and Social Security, volunteers part-time at a free clinic.
Mother.Work_Status: Retired — volunteers 4–8 hours weekly at a community health clinic and helps care for grandchildren occasionally.
Mother.Health_Status: Mild osteoporosis managed with calcium and vitamin D supplements; well-controlled hypothyroidism on levothyroxine; generally active and ambulatory.

Preference_Profile.Cooking: Makes large family meals and traditional desserts for holidays and Sunday family visits; enjoys baking tamales and pan dulce on special occasions.
Preference_Profile.Knitting: Knits in the afternoons while watching TV, typically making scarves and baby blankets for family members.
Preference_Profile.Volunteer_Work: Volunteers at the community clinic one morning per week, helping with basic patient intake and translation when needed.
Preference_Profile.Church_Attendance: Attends Sunday mass or community services most Sundays and participates in parish events.
Preference_Profile.Gardening: Maintains potted herbs and a small flower bed; prefers morning watering and planting seasons (spring/fall).

Sibling_1.Name: Diego Rodriguez
Sibling_1.Career_Status: Senior Mechanical Engineer at an aerospace components supplier in El Segundo. Approximate annual salary: $110,000 USD; modest savings and retirement contributions.
Sibling_1.Work_Status: Busy — hybrid work schedule with frequent project deadlines and occasional travel to supplier facilities.
Sibling_1.Health_Status: Generally healthy, history of mild exercise-induced asthma controlled with an inhaler; no chronic conditions.

Preference_Profile.Cycling: Road cycles for 30–60 miles on weekend mornings, typically joins local group rides on Saturdays.
Preference_Profile.Grilling/BBQ: Hosts neighborhood barbecues and practices grilling techniques on Sunday afternoons when visiting family.
Preference_Profile.Video_Games: Plays competitive/cooperative video games 2–3 weeknights per week for 1–2 hours to relax after work.
Preference_Profile.DIY_Auto: Enjoys basic car maintenance and weekend garage projects once or twice a month.
Preference_Profile.Family_Visits: Plans and visits the parents monthly, often coordinating family get-togethers around holidays.

Partner.Name: Jordan Reyes
Partner.Birthplace: New York City, New York, USA
Partner.Career_Status: Program Manager at a regional transit agency coordinating affordable housing and transit-oriented development. Annual salary approximately $95,000 USD; participates in employer retirement plan.
Partner.Work_Status: Busy — currently leading a community housing project with frequent evening stakeholder meetings and occasional weekend outreach events.
Partner.Health_Status: Generally healthy; lactose intolerant and experiences occasional migraines tied to workload and sleep disruption.

Preference_Profile.Running: Goes for 5–8 km runs early morning, typically 3–4 times per week before work.
Preference_Profile.Reading_Policy: Reads urban planning and policy books/briefs on weeknights, usually 30–60 minutes before bed.
Preference_Profile.Cooking: Prepares mostly vegetarian dinners on weeknights and experiments with seasonal recipes on weekends.
Preference_Profile.Travel: Takes 1–2 short city-break trips per year and prefers exploring museums and public transit systems.
Preference_Profile.Balcony_Gardening: Maintains a balcony herb garden, watering and pruning twice weekly; uses herbs in home cooking.

Contacts_1.Name: Maya Chen
Contacts_1.Career_Status: Senior Software Engineer at a fintech startup in Vancouver. Approximate annual income: 120,000 CAD; has moderate savings and stock options.
Contacts_1.Work_Status: Remote with flexible hours; often available evenings and weekends for social gaming or calls; occasional sprint weeks are busy.
Contacts_1.Health_Status: Good physical health apart from seasonal allergies; practices regular exercise and mindful routines for stress.

Preference_Profile.Online_Gaming: Plays cooperative multiplayer games with the user most Friday nights (around 7–10 PM PT); prefers strategy and co-op titles.
Preference_Profile.Urban_Photography: Goes out for early-morning street photography on weekends when the weather is clear, usually within the city.
Preference_Profile.Hiking: Joins day hikes twice a month around Vancouver when schedules permit, favoring moderate trails and scenic viewpoints.
Preference_Profile.Cooking: Experiments with fusion recipes (Taiwanese-Canadian) on Sunday evenings and shares photos with friends online.
Preference_Profile.Language_Exchange: Practices Spanish for 30–45 minutes weekly via video chat with the user in exchange for helping with Mandarin conversation.

Contacts_2.Name: Evelyn Park
Contacts_2.Residence: San Diego, California, USA (same neighborhood as the user)
Contacts_2.Career_Status: Part-time marketing consultant and freelance social media strategist for local businesses. Annual income varies (approx. $35,000–$50,000); maintains a small emergency fund.
Contacts_2.Work_Status: Normal/flexible — works from home most days, schedules client meetings mid-week and often takes afternoons off for childcare.
Contacts_2.Health_Status: Allergic rhinitis (seasonal); otherwise healthy and active; postpartum recovery completed several years ago.

Preference_Profile.Baking: Bakes with her child on weekend mornings (simple breads, muffins) and brings treats to neighbors occasionally.
Preference_Profile.Yoga: Attends Vinyasa yoga classes twice weekly in the evenings for stress relief.
Preference_Profile.Community_Volunteering: Participates in monthly neighborhood cleanups and the apartment building HOA meetings when available.
Preference_Profile.Coffee_Shop_Work: Works from a local café on Thursdays to meet clients or change scenery; prefers Americano and a window seat.
Preference_Profile.Balcony_Gardening: Maintains a small balcony garden of potted vegetables and herbs, watering early mornings and involving her child in simple plant care.

Static_Conflict_Information.Value: The person's only listed sibling is legally named Rodrigo García and has always used that name publicly.
They are an only child who grew up without siblings and there are no records indicating any siblings.
New York City, New York, USA
Publicly presents and is consistently referred to using she/her pronouns in all professional profiles and interviews.
Born and raised in Guadalajara, Jalisco, Mexico and holds only Mexican citizenship.
Completed a degree and had a long professional career in Mechanical Engineering, including engineering licensure and industry work.
Their mother passed away in 2005 and has been deceased since then.
